name: Mai Tai

spec.method_detail: Shake with crushed ice. Pour unstrained into glass. Float a dark rum on top if desired.

history: Trader Vic (Victor Bergeron) created the Mai Tai in 1944 at his Oakland, CA restaurant. He poured two Jamaican rums from the 1930s vintage and said 'Mai Tai — Roa Ae!' which in Tahitian means 'out of this world — the best.' The recipe has been bastardized over decades into a red sugar bomb; the original is refined and rum-focused. Donn Beach (Don the Beachcomber) disputed credit but the Bergeron version is generally accepted.
